gaps: MISSING: Post-DFU-failure state persistence — No test verifies what the app reports on the Firmware Update screen AFTER a failed DFU has been dismissed/returned to. The bug is that the app falsely says 'up to date' instead of showing the available update again. Existing tests (65459462, 65459463, 65459464) cover the 'Houston' error screen and retry flow, but none check what happens if the user navigates away and comes back, or what the main Firmware Update screen shows after the error is dismissed.

MISSING: Firmware version comparison after failure — No test validates that the app's reported firmware version/status on the Device Settings → Firmware Update screen correctly reflects the ACTUAL device firmware (which did NOT change due to the failed DFU). The bug is a mismatch between the available update and the displayed status.

MISSING: App lifecycle interaction with DFU failure state — No test covers: (a) backgrounding the app during the 'Houston' error screen and returning, (b) killing and relaunching the app after a DFU failure, and (c) checking whether the 'up to date' false state persists across app restarts or clears on reconnect.

MISSING: Bluetooth reconnection after DFU failure — The bug scenario involves disconnecting mid-update. No test verifies the Firmware Update screen state after the device reconnects following a failed DFU. The app might incorrectly cache the 'update succeeded' state on reconnect.

MISSING: Device asymmetry — No test differentiates between headset types (e.g., Voyager Surround 85 vs. Free 60 vs. Sync 20) for post-DFU-failure behavior. Different devices may have different firmware update mechanisms or state reporting that could affect whether the 'up to date' false positive occurs.

MISSING: Multiple failure modes — Existing tests only cover 'headset power-off' and 'internet disconnection' as failure causes. The bug scenario also mentions 'disconnect mid-update' and 'timeout'. No test covers timeout-based DFU failure or Bluetooth disconnection (without power-off) as the failure trigger.

MISSING: Retry option persistence — No test verifies that the retry option remains available after: (a) navigating to another app screen and back, (b) rotating the device, (c) receiving a phone call during the error state.

generated_testcases.title: Post-DFU-failure: Firmware Update screen shows available update after navigating away and back (Voyager Surround 85)
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Surround 85) has an available firmware update. Ensure device is connected via Bluetooth.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Tap 'Update' to start DFU.
During DFU, trigger failure by disconnecting Bluetooth from phone settings (do NOT power off device).
Observe 'Houston' error screen appears.
Tap 'Dismiss' or navigate back to Device Settings screen.
Navigate back to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware status text and available update banner.
generated_testcases.expected: Firmware Update screen shows the available update banner again (e.g., 'Update available: v2.5.1'). Status does NOT read 'Up to date'. Actual (If Bug): App falsely displays 'Your device is up to date' despite device still running the older firmware version.

generated_testcases.title: Post-DFU-failure: Firmware version mismatch validation after failed update (Free 60)
steps: Precondition: Free 60 connected. Note current firmware version on device (e.g., v1.2.0). Available update is v1.4.0.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Verify 'Update available: v1.4.0' is shown.
Initiate DFU. During update, trigger failure by turning device off.
After error screen appears, dismiss it.
Navigate to: Device → About Device
Note the reported firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the status text.
generated_testcases.expected: About Device shows firmware version v1.2.0 (unchanged from pre-update). Firmware Update screen shows 'Update available: v1.4.0'. Actual (If Bug): App reports 'Up to date' or shows current version as v1.4.0 despite device still on v1.2.0.

generated_testcases.title: App lifecycle: Background and return during DFU failure 'Houston' error screen (Sync 20)
steps: Precondition: Sync 20 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. Trigger failure by disconnecting Bluetooth.
Wait for 'Houston' error screen to appear.
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from recent apps.
Observe the current screen and state.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
generated_testcases.expected: App returns to the 'Houston' error screen OR the Firmware Update screen showing the available update. After navigating to Firmware Update, the available update banner is displayed. Actual (If Bug): App dismisses error state on background/foreground and shows 'Up to date'.

generated_testcases.title: App lifecycle: Force-kill and relaunch after DFU failure (Voyager Surround 85)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Surround 85 connected. Available firmware update v2.0.0. Device currently on v1.8.0.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. Trigger failure by disconnecting Bluetooth mid-update.
Wait for 'Houston' error screen.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps).
Reopen the app and wait for Bluetooth reconnection.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware status and available update.
generated_testcases.expected: After relaunch and reconnect, Firmware Update screen shows 'Update available: v2.0.0'. Device About page shows v1.8.0. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Up to date' after relaunch, incorrectly caching the failed update as successful.

generated_testcases.title: Bluetooth reconnection after DFU failure: Firmware Update screen state (Free 60)
steps: Precondition: Free 60 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. During update, turn off the device Bluetooth (or power off device).
Wait for 'Houston' error screen. Dismiss it.
Turn device back on and wait for automatic Bluetooth reconnection.
Observe the app screen after reconnection.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware status.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth reconnection, Firmware Update screen shows the available update banner. The app does NOT display 'Up to date'. Actual (If Bug): App incorrectly caches 'update succeeded' state on reconnect and shows 'Your device is up to date'.

generated_testcases.title: Device asymmetry: Post-DFU-failure behavior comparison (Voyager Surround 85 vs. Free 60 vs. Sync 20)
steps: Precondition: Three test devices — Voyager Surround 85, Free 60, Sync 20 — each connected to separate test phones. Each has an available firmware update.
For each device: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update. Note the available update version.
For each device: Start DFU and trigger failure by disconnecting Bluetooth mid-update.
For each device: Dismiss the 'Houston' error screen.
For each device: Navigate back to Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Record the displayed status for each device type.
For each device: Force-kill app, relaunch, reconnect, and re-check Firmware Update screen.
Compare results across all three device types.
generated_testcases.expected: All three device types (Voyager Surround 85, Free 60, Sync 20) show the available update banner after DFU failure, both after dismiss and after app relaunch. Actual (If Bug): One or more device types falsely display 'Up to date' while others correctly show the available update, indicating device-specific state caching logic.

generated_testcases.title: DFU failure by timeout: Firmware Update screen state after timeout error (Sync 20)
steps: Precondition: Sync 20 connected. Available firmware update. Ensure network connectivity is stable.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. During update, move device out of Bluetooth range (e.g., 30+ meters away) to cause timeout.
Wait for error screen to appear (may take 2-5 minutes).
Bring device back into range and wait for reconnection.
Dismiss the error screen.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware status.
generated_testcases.expected: Firmware Update screen shows the available update banner after timeout-based failure. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Up to date' after a timeout failure, treating the incomplete update as successful.

generated_testcases.title: DFU failure by Bluetooth disconnection (no power-off): Firmware Update screen state (Voyager Surround 85)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Surround 85 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. During update, go to phone Bluetooth settings and 'Forget' or disconnect the device (do NOT power off the headset).
Wait for 'Houston' error screen.
Re-pair and reconnect the device via Bluetooth settings.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware status.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth disconnection (without power-off) and re-pairing, Firmware Update screen shows the available update banner. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Up to date' after re-pairing, incorrectly treating the disconnected mid-update state as a completed update.

generated_testcases.title: Retry option persistence after navigating to another app screen and back (Free 60)
steps: Precondition: Free 60 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. Trigger failure by turning device off.
Wait for 'Houston' error screen with retry option.
Tap 'Dismiss' or navigate back to Device Settings.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings
Navigate back to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe if the retry option (Update button) is available.
generated_testcases.expected: Firmware Update screen shows the 'Update' button with the available firmware version. User can tap to retry the update. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Up to date' with no retry option, or the Update button is missing/greyed out.

generated_testcases.title: Retry option persistence after device rotation during error state (Sync 20)
steps: Precondition: Sync 20 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. Trigger failure by disconnecting Bluetooth.
Wait for 'Houston' error screen with retry option.
Rotate device to landscape orientation.
Rotate device back to portrait orientation.
Observe the screen state after rotation.
Tap retry option if available.
Observe behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: After rotation, the error screen or Firmware Update screen persists with the retry option still available. Tapping retry initiates a new DFU attempt. Actual (If Bug): Rotation causes the error state to be dismissed and replaced with 'Up to date', or the retry button disappears after config change.

generated_testcases.title: Retry option persistence after incoming phone call during DFU failure state (Voyager Surround 85)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Surround 85 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. Trigger failure by disconnecting Bluetooth.
Wait for 'Houston' error screen with retry option.
Receive an incoming phone call on the test phone (do not answer — let it ring and go to voicemail or reject).
After call ends, observe the app screen.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update if needed.
Observe if retry option is available.
generated_testcases.expected: After the incoming call interruption, the Firmware Update screen still shows the available update with retry option. Actual (If Bug): The incoming call causes the app to reset the DFU state, showing 'Up to date' instead of the available update.

generated_testcases.title: Immediate state trigger: Kill app IMMEDIATELY after DFU failure before dismissing error (Free 60)
steps: Precondition: Free 60 connected. Available firmware update.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Start DFU. Trigger failure by powering off device.
Wait for 'Houston' error screen to appear.
DO NOT dismiss or navigate away. IMMEDIATELY force-kill the app.
Power on the device and wait for Bluetooth reconnection.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware status.
generated_testcases.expected: After force-kill during the error screen (before any dismiss action), relaunch shows the available update banner on the Firmware Update screen. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Up to date' because the error state was never formally dismissed/acknowledged, causing the app to default to a cached 'success' state.
